pending.body: In `success_reconciliation.rs` `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success`, the function filters mappings with `m.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id)` at line 722, which means legacy mappings with `run_id: None` are excluded from post-run verification entirely. If a bead has unverified PE mappings from a legacy run (before run_id tracking was added), they will never be verified or receive comments during success reconciliation. Consider whether legacy mappings should be included in verification when no run_id-bearing mappings exist for the bead.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:278-305` intentionally allows reviewers to use short-form bead IDs like `8.5.3`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:648-655` preserves those values when they are listed in the prompt. But `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:78-97` only retries canonical -> short aliasing after `br show` fails; it never expands a short ID back to the canonical `9ni.8.5.3` form. In this repo, `br show 9ni.8.5.3` succeeds while `br show 8.5.3` returns `ISSUE_NOT_FOUND`, so a valid planned-elsewhere mapping can be downgraded to a stale warning, persisted with `mapped_bead_verified=false`, and never receive the optional comment.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7614-7638` treats failure to append the planned-elsewhere round sentinel as a warning and continues. That sentinel is the only thing that lets `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2599-2627` supersede older mappings when the latest completion round has zero planned-elsewhere findings. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:991-1024` can only reconstruct accepted mappings from the final-review aggregate, so it cannot repair a missing zero-PE sentinel. After a single sentinel-write failure, stale mappings from an earlier round remain authoritative in milestone state even though the latest accepted round cleared them.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 24 -> Plan and Implement
